About me

Code, Teach,
Repeat.

I'm a Computer Science sophomore at Wright State University — originally from Nepal. I came here not knowing exactly what to expect, and that experience is what drives me to create tools that make the path easier for others coming after me.

Right now I'm coding, coaching, and constantly learning. I work as an Peer Academic Coach at WSU — because I think the best way to really understand something is to teach it. I'm actively looking for an internship where I can contribute from day one.

Projects

Things I've Built

01 — Featured

International Student Academic Support

A dedicated resource hub for international students navigating US university systems. Designed from lived experience — bridging knowledge gaps from day one.

HTMLCSSGitHub Pages
View Project →
02

Choose Your Own Adventure Game

A two-player, interactive console-based adventure game built for CS 1180 at Wright State University. Players choose tunnels, solve puzzles, and make story decisions that determine whether they survive.

Java OOP Console App
View Project →
03

Simon Says – Memory Game

A Java console recreation of the classic Simon Says memory game. Players memorize and repeat increasingly longer color or digit sequences, with difficulty selection, dynamic scoring, and a replay option.

Java Game Logic Console App
View Project →
04

Spelling Bee – Word Validation Game

A Java console-based Spelling Bee game that loads a word list from an external file and validates user input against it. Built with file I/O and in-memory lookups for fast word checking.

Java File I/O Console App
View Project →
05

More Coming Soon

Currently building new projects. Reach out to collaborate.
